capture.content:
# Field identity (__eq__/__hash__/__lt__) — creation_counter conflates abstract-base fields

- `Field.__eq__`, `__lt__`, and `__hash__` are defined on the Field base class in `django/db/models/fields/__init__.py` (~lines 516-529) and historically compared ONLY `self.creation_counter`.
- Gotcha: fields inherited from an abstract base model are cloned onto each concrete subclass but keep the SAME creation_counter. So `B._meta.get_field('myfield') == C._meta.get_field('myfield')` was True and the fields collided in sets/dicts — anything keyed on field identity alone (dedup sets, dict keys) silently conflates same-named fields across sibling models.
- Fix pattern: include the bound model in identity — `__eq__` requires equal creation_counter AND same `field.model`, using `getattr(self, 'model', None)` because UNBOUND fields (not yet contributed to a class) have no `.model` attribute; `__hash__` must change in lockstep with `__eq__` (hash the tuple).
- Ordering-stability lesson: when adding a tie-breaker to `__lt__`, keep the ORIGINAL key (creation_counter) as the primary sort key and the new key (model) secondary — ordering by model first re-ordered cases unaffected by the collision and broke an existing test. General rule: extend comparison dunders so pre-existing orderings are preserved for all previously-distinguishable pairs.
- Symptom signature: "same-named fields from different models compare equal / de-duplicate in a set" → the fix lives in the Field dunders in `django/db/models/fields/__init__.py`, not in any consumer (migrations autodetector, forms, sets built by user code).

## Localization lesson
Single-file fix in `django/db/models/fields/__init__.py`; the agent's 1-file localization matched the real fix footprint exactly. Another confirmation of the fix-at-the-definition rule: identity/comparison semantics belong on the base class where the dunders are defined, not at the many call sites that compare or hash fields. Also note: all three dunders (`__eq__`, `__hash__`, `__lt__`) must be adjusted together — Python requires eq/hash consistency, and a partial fix reintroduces the bug through the untouched dunder.
